steps.title: Convert 4% annual increase to a growth factor
steps.content:
When the population increases by 4% of the previous year's population, each new year we have the old population plus 4% of it:
$$p_{\text{new}} = p_{\text{old}} + 0.04 \cdot p_{\text{old}} = p_{\text{old}} (1 + 0.04) = 1.04 \cdot p_{\text{old}}$$
The growth factor is 1.04.

steps.title: Write the exponential model
steps.content:
Starting from 15,000 in 2005, multiply by 1.04 for each year that passes:
  • $x = 1$: $p = 15,000 \cdot 1.04$
  • $x = 2$: $p = 15,000 \cdot 1.04^2$
  • $x = 3$: $p = 15,000 \cdot 1.04^3$
The general pattern is:
$$p = 15,000(1.04)^x$$
This matches choice D — D is correct.
